description: Lock Military Waystations to Achaemenid road/postal waystations and Roman Military Road Waystations to the later cursus-publicus relay-stage scope.

checks.reason: The canonical node is anchored to source-backed Achaemenid Persian posthouses and Royal Road stations in the 6th century BCE.
The corrected Persian waystation scope is carried by roads and postal relays; the broad logistics node is later and too general as a direct prerequisite.
Royal-road stations are defined along imperial routes, but roads remain enabling rather than a universal hard requirement for every future waystation variant.
Postal relays carry the horse-change and courier mechanism for the source-backed Persian scope.
The neighboring node is now scoped to Roman cursus-publicus relay stages rather than the earlier Achaemenid Royal Road station concept.
